label: PUBLIC_SKILL_AUTHORING_API_CONTRACT_DRIFT
output_md:
## skill_boundary_brief.md
Create a reusable Soulforge skill for public-safe API contract drift review. Track only redacted, synthetic, or public-safe inputs. Keep runtime packets, private logs, credentials, customer endpoint names, and `_workspaces` files out of tracked canon.

## skill_package_draft.md
Name: `api_contract_drift_check`.
Purpose: review a redacted API contract drift packet, separate confirmed evidence from assumptions, identify likely owners and blockers, and produce a bounded remediation plan.
Core outputs: evidence summary, assumptions, owner handoff, remediation order, boundary warnings.

## skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Allowed bundle: `skill.yaml`, `README.md`, optional `codex/SKILL.md`, and public-safe templates or checklists. A helper script is acceptable only if it works on synthetic or redacted packet files and does not embed project-specific endpoint names.

## skill_boundary_review.md
Do not copy actual API specs, production logs, credentials, private incident details, or runtime workspace files into tracked skill canon. The skill should treat those as runtime inputs only and should warn when packet contents are incomplete or redacted.

## skill_install_sync_request.md
Prepare a local install handoff note for the mirror sync step. Do not claim sync was performed. The package should tell the operator what to copy into the installed mirror and what to verify after sync.

## skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke with a tiny synthetic packet containing two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route handler note, and one failing test name. Verify the skill returns evidence, assumptions, owner handoff, remediation order, and boundary warnings.

## skill_release_review.md
Release only after the package stays public-safe, the smoke path works on synthetic/redacted input, and the documentation makes the boundary explicit. If any tracked artifact starts to encode private endpoint names or live packet data, block release.
